On 2026-01-19, the FCA flagged that six weeks of order state transition records for regulated instruments were non-compliant with COBS 11.2A audit trail requirements. The gap stemmed from a mismatch between MJ's event-sourced architecture (ADR-014, approved ~September 2025) and Bird's pre-existing domain rule requiring synchronous, immediately-durable state transitions committed to the compliance record store

trials.justification: All pass criteria met. Preserving the Learning section contains 6 named artifacts (exceeds the 3 minimum). Artifact 1 is an ADR-014 addendum owned by MJ. Artifact 2 is a domain rule update with arch-constraint tags owned by Bird. Artifact 4 is Kobe's review checklist update owned by Kobe. Each artifact has type, detailed content description, and named owner. Contributing Factors includes Kobe's checklist gap (3b), terminology mismatch (3a), Bird not tagged (3c), and no audit trail completeness monitoring (3d). Multiple PROCESS-tagged Forward Commitments present. Tone is consistently non-prosecutorial — explicitly states Kobe's omission 'is not negligent' and frames all factors as systemic, not individual failures. observations.text: Six artifacts in Preserving the Learning — double the required minimum — each with type, location, content, rationale, and named owner
